rings.name: Assess
rings.description: Explore to understand how it might affect you

rings.name: Hold
rings.description: Proceed with caution in new projects

blips.name: Claude Code
blips.description: CLI-native AI coding agent that handles multi-file edits, git ops, and complex refactors autonomously. The terminal-first approach outperforms IDE-embedded copilots for large-scale changes.

blips.name: RAG Pipelines
blips.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is now the default architecture for production LLM applications. Embedding + vector search + generation is a solved pattern.

blips.name: Cloudflare Workers
blips.description: Edge-native serverless compute with sub-millisecond cold starts. D1, KV, R2, and Queues form a complete platform without managing infrastructure.

blips.name: Cursor
blips.description: AI-first code editor built on VS Code. Tab completion and multi-file edits make it the productivity default for greenfield projects.

blips.name: Supabase
blips.description: Open-source Firebase alternative with Postgres, auth, realtime, and edge functions. Mature enough for production SaaS with excellent DX.

blips.name: LangChain
blips.description: Popular LLM orchestration framework, but growing complexity and abstraction overhead push teams toward simpler alternatives like direct SDK calls or LangGraph.

blips.name: Bun
blips.description: All-in-one JavaScript runtime challenging Node.js with dramatically faster startup, built-in bundler, and npm compatibility. Production adoption is accelerating.

blips.name: Vercel v0
blips.description: AI-powered UI generation that produces React components from natural language. Useful for rapid prototyping but generated code often needs significant cleanup.

blips.name: Stripe Billing
blips.description: The default choice for SaaS subscription management. Handles pricing tiers, trials, upgrades, and invoicing with minimal custom code.

blips.name: Llama 3.1
blips.description: Meta's open-weight model family. The 70B variant approaches GPT-4 level on many benchmarks while running on commodity hardware. Key for teams needing data sovereignty.

blips.name: Docker Compose for Dev
blips.description: Still the most reliable way to replicate production-like environments locally. Docker Desktop alternatives (OrbStack, Colima) improve the DX significantly.

blips.name: Tailwind CSS v4
blips.description: Major rewrite using Rust-based engine. Lightning CSS integration, automatic content detection, and CSS-first configuration reduce setup friction dramatically.

blips.name: Anthropic Claude API
blips.description: 200K context window, tool use, and computer use capabilities. Leading choice for complex agentic workflows requiring long-context reasoning.

blips.name: Kubernetes (for small teams)
blips.description: Operational complexity remains prohibitive for teams under 5 engineers. Managed alternatives (Railway, Fly.io, Cloudflare) handle most workloads with far less overhead.

blips.name: GitHub Actions
blips.description: De facto CI/CD for GitHub-hosted projects. Marketplace actions and reusable workflows cover most automation needs without dedicated DevOps tooling.

blips.name: Vite
blips.description: The build tool that killed webpack for frontend development. Sub-second HMR, Rollup-based production builds, and framework-agnostic design make it the universal default.

blips.name: OpenAI Assistants API
blips.description: Managed agent framework with file search, code interpreter, and function calling. Convenient but opaque — debugging failures is difficult and vendor lock-in is real.

blips.name: AI Code Review Agents
blips.description: Automated PR review bots (CodeRabbit, Ellipsis, Copilot Review) catch bugs and style issues. Not a replacement for human review but a valuable first pass.

blips.name: Deno 2
blips.description: Secure-by-default runtime with npm compatibility added in v2. Compelling for new projects, but ecosystem gaps and migration cost keep adoption cautious.

blips.name: Multi-Agent Systems
blips.description: Orchestrating multiple AI agents to solve complex tasks collaboratively. Promising but reliability and debugging remain unsolved at scale.

blips.name: Fly.io
blips.description: Deploy containers globally with built-in load balancing and Postgres. Great developer experience for teams that need more control than serverless but less than Kubernetes.

blips.name: tRPC
blips.description: End-to-end type-safe APIs without code generation. Excellent for TypeScript monorepos where client and server share a codebase.

blips.name: Stable Diffusion XL
blips.description: Open-source image generation competitive with DALL-E 3. Self-hosting enables fine-tuning and avoids content policy restrictions for legitimate creative use.

blips.name: PlanetScale
blips.description: Serverless MySQL platform. Free tier removal and Vitess complexity push small teams toward Supabase (Postgres) or Turso (SQLite). Still strong for MySQL-dependent workloads.

blips.name: Turborepo
blips.description: Monorepo build system with intelligent caching. Simpler than Nx for TypeScript projects. Remote caching via Vercel speeds up CI significantly.

blips.name: Hono
blips.description: Ultrafast web framework designed for edge runtimes. Works on Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun, and Node. Becoming the Express.js successor for modern stacks.

blips.name: Fine-tuning LLMs
blips.description: Custom model training is expensive and often unnecessary. RAG + prompt engineering solves most domain-specific needs. Reserve fine-tuning for truly unique data patterns.

blips.name: Neon Postgres
blips.description: Serverless Postgres with branch-like database forking. Scale-to-zero billing and instant provisioning make it compelling for dev/staging environments.

blips.name: Biome
blips.description: Rust-based linter and formatter replacing ESLint + Prettier with a single tool. 10-100x faster, but plugin ecosystem is still catching up.

blips.name: Toss Payments API
blips.description: Korea's leading payment gateway with clean REST API design. Handles card payments, virtual accounts, and mobile payments. Essential for any Korean-market SaaS.
